title: Incident Response Readiness Audit
summary: Assess whether security incidents are triaged, escalated, contained, and documented quickly enough to support resilient response.
objective: Assess whether the incident response process for security events is designed and operating effectively across detection, escalation, containment, and post-incident review.

controls.name: Timely Triage of Critical Alerts
controls.description: Critical security alerts should be reviewed within the defined response window.

controls.name: Formal Major Incident Escalation
controls.description: Material security incidents should be escalated according to the major incident playbook.

controls.name: Documented Containment Actions
controls.description: Containment decisions should be documented with timestamps and responsible owners.

controls.name: Post-Incident Review Completion
controls.description: Significant incidents should conclude with lessons learned and action tracking.

evidence.title: Phishing Incident Timeline
evidence.content: Timeline for the payroll phishing event shows the malicious inbox rule alert triggered at 08:11, but analyst review was not recorded until 11:46.

evidence.title: SIEM Critical Alert Queue
evidence.content: Queue export shows four critical alerts marked open beyond SLA, including one impossible-travel event affecting the CFO mailbox.

evidence.title: Containment Chat Transcript
evidence.content: The response chat confirms the team reset the user's password and blocked forwarding rules, but no ticket captured the exact containment owner or timestamps.

evidence.title: Major Incident Register
evidence.content: The major incident register does not include the phishing event even though the service impact threshold would have required escalation.

evidence.title: Lessons Learned Tracker
evidence.content: The tracker contains an open placeholder for the payroll phishing review with no owner and no completion date.

evidence.title: Incident Response Procedure
evidence.content: The procedure defines triage SLAs, major incident escalation criteria, containment documentation, and mandatory post-incident review for material events.

evidence.title: On-Call Roster
evidence.content: The weekly on-call roster shows primary and secondary coverage for security operations across the month, with no open shift gaps or unassigned standby periods.

evidence.title: Executive Status Email
evidence.content: A status email sent to leadership says the phishing event was being handled as a major incident, but the incident register and bridge log were never updated to match that statement.

issues.title: Critical Security Alert Not Triaged Within SLA
issues.description: A critical phishing-related alert remained unreviewed beyond the defined triage window.
issues.recommendation: Enforce alert queue monitoring and escalation for critical events breaching response SLA.

issues.title: Material Security Event Not Escalated as Major Incident
issues.description: A significant phishing incident was not recorded or escalated through the major incident process.
issues.recommendation: Apply major incident criteria consistently and require formal register entry for qualifying events.

issues.title: Containment Actions Not Fully Documented
issues.description: Containment activity occurred, but ownership and exact timing were not formally captured.
issues.recommendation: Record containment actions in a formal ticket with timestamps, ownership, and decision rationale.

issues.title: Post-Incident Review Not Completed
issues.description: The incident lacked a completed lessons-learned review and tracked remediation actions.
issues.recommendation: Require post-incident review completion with assigned owners and due dates for follow-up actions.

inbox.subject: Kickoff: Incident Response Readiness
inbox.preview: Test whether the response process is actually fast and disciplined during real events.
inbox.body: We are looking for operational readiness, not just a policy binder. Focus on whether alerts are triaged quickly, incidents are escalated properly, and response actions are documented well enough to stand up after the fact.

inbox.subject: Case material attached
inbox.preview: Sharing the alert queue, incident timeline, and current response procedure.
inbox.body: The phishing case was messy because several alerts landed the same morning. I have attached the queue extract and response timeline to help with your review.

interviewPrompts.question: Why was the payroll phishing alert reviewed so late?
interviewPrompts.answer: We had multiple critical alerts that morning and only one analyst covering the queue. The alert stayed open longer than it should have.

interviewPrompts.question: Was the event escalated as a major incident?
interviewPrompts.answer: No. We treated it as a contained mailbox issue, even though the service impact and executive exposure would normally meet the escalation threshold.

interviewPrompts.question: Were containment actions documented anywhere formal?
interviewPrompts.answer: The team acted quickly in chat and on the admin console, but the formal incident ticket was not updated with the detailed containment steps.

interviewPrompts.question: Was a lessons-learned review completed after the incident?
interviewPrompts.answer: We scheduled one but never closed it out. The action tracker still shows it as pending without an owner.

interviewPrompts.question: What was service desk told during the event?
interviewPrompts.answer: Support was told to watch for mailbox complaints, but no formal major incident bridge was opened, so communication stayed informal.

interviewPrompts.question: Did leadership receive a major-incident update for the phishing event?
interviewPrompts.answer: They received a status note that made it sound like one, but the formal register entry never happened and the bridge log stayed empty.

closeout.managementMessage: The incident response process is defined, but execution during live events is inconsistent. Triage speed, escalation discipline, and follow-up documentation need improvement to support credible response readiness.
closeout.auditorReflection: Incident audits reward timeline thinking. The real story usually emerges when you line up alert time, response time, escalation choices, and what was never documented.
